description: Staying on the rollback journal makes a writer block all readers, serializing an otherwise read-heavy workload.
body: Failure mode: the database uses the default rollback journal, so while a write transaction is open all readers are blocked and a reader blocks the writer, collapsing throughput for a concurrent read-heavy app. Detection signals: readers stall whenever a write is in progress; throughput far below expectation under mixed load; journal_mode never set to WAL. Do not raise timeouts to mask the serialization. Safer fallback: enable PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL so readers and the single writer proceed concurrently, keeping the -wal/-shm files alongside the database.
